# Ben Gramigna — Personal Landing Page

*Replace this heading with your own name if reusing this template.*

## Description

*2–4 sentences describing what the project does, why it exists, and who it is for.*

A professional personal landing page for Ben Gramigna, a Business Analytics and Information Systems student at the University of Iowa graduating in May 2026. The site goes beyond a traditional resume to tell a fuller professional story — showcasing Ben's technical skills, project work, leadership experience, and personality in a single, recruiter-ready page. It is designed for business-oriented employers in analytics, finance, and general business roles who want to quickly understand his background and capabilities.

## Features

*List the key features a visitor or recruiter would notice when using the site.*

- **Hero section** with name, professional tagline, and circular headshot
- **About section** with personal bio and key academic stats (GPA, degree, graduation date)
- **Technical skills** organized by Tools and Analytical Techniques with styled pill tags
- **Project showcase** featuring three detailed project cards with technology labels
- **Professional timeline** covering two internships and fraternity leadership roles
- **Direct contact links** for email, LinkedIn, and GitHub — all opening to real profiles
- **Fully responsive** layout that adapts cleanly to mobile, tablet, and desktop
- **Accessible design** meeting WCAG 2.2 AA standards with proper focus indicators

## Tech Stack

*List every technology used and its role in the project.*

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| HTML5 | Page structure and semantic markup |
| CSS3 | Styling, layout (Grid & Flexbox), and responsive design |
| Google Fonts (Inter) | Clean, professional sans-serif typography |
| CSS Custom Properties | Consistent color palette and design tokens across the stylesheet |
| Python `http.server` | Local development server for previewing the site |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

*Everything needed before running the project locally.*

- A modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge)
- [Python 3](https://www.python.org/downloads/) — for running the local development server (pre-installed on macOS and most Linux systems)
- [Git](https://git-scm.com/downloads) — to clone the repository

### Installation

*Step-by-step instructions to run the project on your machine.*

1. Clone the repository:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/bengramigna/3300-landing-page.git
```

2. Navigate into the project folder:

```bash
cd 3300-landing-page
```

3. Start a local web server:

```bash
python3 -m http.server 5000
```

4. Open your browser and visit:

```
http://localhost:5000
```

## Usage

*How to use the application once it is running.*

The landing page is a single-page site — scroll down through each section (About, Skills, Projects, Experience, Contact) or use the sticky navigation bar at the top to jump directly to any section. All links in the Contact section open in a new browser tab. No login or configuration is required.

To customize this template for your own use, update the content in `index.html` with your personal details, and adjust colors and fonts in `stylesheet.css` using the CSS custom properties defined at the top of the file (`:root` block).

## Project Structure

*Overview of the main files and folders and what each one does.*

```
3300-landing-page/
├── index.html              # Main HTML file — all page sections and content
├── stylesheet.css          # External stylesheet — layout, colors, typography, responsive rules
├── images/
│   └── Composite02.JPG    # Professional headshot displayed in the hero section
├── PRD.md                  # Product Requirements Document — goals, audience, content spec
├── Combined_PRD.md         # Merged PRD + STANDARDS reference document
├── Ben-Gramigna resume.pdf # Source resume used to populate page content
└── README.md               # This file
```

## Changelog

*A record of changes made to the project.*

### v1.0.0 — 2026-03-23

**Initial release**

- Built complete single-page landing page from scratch using HTML5 and CSS3
- Sticky navigation bar with smooth-scroll anchor links to all sections
- Hero section with circular headshot, name, tagline, and two CTA buttons
- About section with bio copy and academic stat cards (GPA, degree, minor, graduation)
- Skills section with two-column pill tag layout separating Tools from Analytical Techniques
- Three project cards: Sport Difficulty Regression, Financial Dashboard Automation, Data Visualization & Analysis
- Professional experience timeline covering Naperville Country Club, Jonathan Wesley Inc, and Phi Gamma Delta
- Contact section with email, LinkedIn, and GitHub icon cards linking to real profiles
- Fully responsive layout with breakpoints at 900px and 600px
- WCAG 2.2 AA accessible with semantic HTML5, ARIA labels, and visible focus indicators

## Known Issues / To-Do

*Things that are incomplete, could be improved, or planned for a future version.*

- [ ] Add a downloadable resume button (PDF link in the hero or contact section)
- [ ] Add a working contact form so visitors can message Ben directly without opening an email client
- [ ] Improve mobile navigation — current horizontal nav links can crowd on very small screens; a hamburger menu would improve usability
- [ ] Add Open Graph meta tags (`og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`) so the page previews correctly when shared on LinkedIn or other platforms
- [ ] Add project screenshots or live demo links to the project cards for richer context

## Roadmap

*Potential features and improvements for a future version.*

- **Dark mode toggle** — allow visitors to switch between light and dark themes via a CSS variable swap and a toggle button
- **Scroll-triggered animations** — subtle fade-in or slide-up effects as sections enter the viewport using the Intersection Observer API
- **Blog or case study section** — a simple section to share write-ups, class projects, or industry analysis as Ben's portfolio grows post-graduation
- **GitHub activity feed** — embed a live view of recent GitHub contributions to demonstrate consistent technical work
- **Analytics integration** — add lightweight, privacy-friendly page view tracking (e.g., Plausible or GoatCounter) to measure visitor engagement against the PRD's success metrics

## Contributing

*How to suggest changes or improvements.*

This is a personal portfolio project, but feedback and suggestions are welcome. To propose a change:

1. Fork the repository on GitHub
2. Create a new branch for your change:
   ```bash
   git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
   ```
3. Make your edits to `index.html` or `stylesheet.css`
4. Commit with a clear, descriptive message:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "Add: brief description of your change"
   ```
5. Push your branch and open a pull request against `main`, describing what you changed and why
